compliance.upload_hint: PDF files only. Text is extracted locally — the file never leaves the server.

quickstart.title: Get started in 4 steps
quickstart.step1_desc: Go to «Cases» and create a new forensic file. You can add a title and descriptive notes.
quickstart.step2_desc: Inside the case, upload one or more files (PNG/JPG images or PDF, max 20 MB).
quickstart.step3_desc: Open the Document Agent, attach the document and describe what you want to analyse in natural language.
quickstart.step4_desc: Upload the report as a PDF in the «ENFSI Compliance» section to verify adherence to BPM guidelines.

features.title: Features
features.projects_desc: Create and manage forensic files. Upload documents, run structured analyses (HTR, signature, NER, graphology, dating) and download results.
features.agent_desc: AI chatbot with 13 specialised forensic tools. Interact in natural language: transcribe handwritten text, detect and verify signatures, analyse layout, identify the writer and more.
features.rag_desc: Chatbot powered by a forensic knowledge base (30+ pre-loaded documents). Query forensic literature, ENFSI guidelines and graphological parameters.
features.compliance_desc: Upload a forensic report as PDF and automatically verify compliance against the 20 requirements of the ENFSI BPM (Best Practice Manual, Ed.03, section 13.2).
features.admin_desc: User management (roles: Administrator, Examiner, Read-only) and activity log with filters by action and user. Accessible to administrators only.

models.title: AI Models and fallback logic
models.intro: GraphoLab uses four categories of AI models, all configurable by the user in the Settings dialog.
models.llm_desc: Used for agent reasoning, RAG response generation, pipeline synthesis and ENFSI compliance checking. Example: qwen3:4b, llama3.2:3b.
models.vlm_desc: Used to analyse tables, figures and images attached to the agent. Example: qwen3-vl:8b, minicpm-v:8b.
models.ocr_desc: Used for handwritten text transcription. Options: easyocr (default), trocr, paddleocr, or the configured VLM model.
models.embed_desc: Used to index the knowledge base and enable semantic search. Example: nomic-embed-text (local), text-embedding-3-small (OpenAI).
models.openai_desc: If you configure an OpenAI API key in Settings, you can choose GPT models as an alternative to local Ollama models. The key is encrypted in the database.
models.fallback_1: If Ollama is unreachable, all AI functions are disabled and a warning is shown.
models.fallback_2: Models can be changed in real time from the sidebar without restarting the server.
models.fallback_3: The application works entirely offline without requiring OpenAI.

agent_examples.title: Agent command examples
agent_examples.intro: The Document Agent understands natural language. Here are example commands grouped by function.
agent_examples.transcription_1: Transcribe the handwritten text in this document
agent_examples.transcription_2: Extract all named entities (people, places, dates) from the document
agent_examples.signature_1: Detect signatures present in the document
agent_examples.signature_2: Verify whether the two signatures belong to the same person
agent_examples.signature_3: Identify the probable author of the handwriting
agent_examples.layout_1: Analyse the document layout and describe its structure
agent_examples.layout_2: Extract the text in reading order
agent_examples.layout_3: Analyse the table present in the document
agent_examples.layout_4: Interpret the chart or figure in the document
agent_examples.graphology_2: Analyse the pressure and inclination of the handwriting
agent_examples.dating_1: Extract and normalise the dates present in the document
agent_examples.kb_1: What does forensic literature say about signature verification?
agent_examples.kb_2: Query the knowledge base on ENFSI graphological parameters
agent_examples.compliance_1: Check this report's compliance against ENFSI standards

faq.title: FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Do I need an internet connection?
faq.a1: No. GraphoLab works entirely locally with Ollama. The only exception is if you configure an OpenAI key, which requires connectivity for API calls.
faq.q2: Which Ollama models do I need to install?
faq.a2: At least one LLM (e.g. qwen3:4b) and one embedding model (e.g. nomic-embed-text). For visual analysis a VLM is also recommended (e.g. qwen3-vl:8b).
faq.a3: GraphoLab uses a Siamese neural network (SigNet) trained on forensic handwriting. It compares two signature images and returns a similarity score with a verdict.
faq.q4: Are my documents sent to external services?
faq.a4: No, unless you use OpenAI. With Ollama all processing is local and files never leave the server.
faq.q5: Can I add my own documents to the knowledge base?
faq.a5: Yes. In the «AI Consultant» section you can upload additional PDFs or text files that will be indexed and made available for RAG queries.
faq.q6: What if the agent analysis does not produce the expected results?
faq.a6: Try rephrasing the command more specifically, or check that the selected LLM model is appropriate. Larger models produce more reliable results.
faq.q7: How many ENFSI requirements are checked?
faq.a7: The system checks 20 requirements from the ENFSI BPM (Best Practice Manual, Ed.03, section 13.2).

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.enfsi_def: European Network of Forensic Science Institutes. European body that defines best practices for forensic examination.
glossary.bpm_def: Best Practice Manual. ENFSI document containing guidelines for forensic graphology reports.
glossary.htr_def: Handwritten Text Recognition. Automatic recognition of handwritten text using neural networks.
glossary.signet_def: Siamese neural network specialised in comparing and verifying autograph signatures.
glossary.rag_def: Retrieval-Augmented Generation. Technique that enriches LLM responses with documents retrieved from the knowledge base.
glossary.hog_def: Histogram of Oriented Gradients. Visual feature descriptor used for writer identification.
glossary.vlm_def: Vision-Language Model. AI model capable of processing both text and images.
glossary.llm_def: Large Language Model. AI model for natural language understanding and generation.
glossary.ocr_def: Optical Character Recognition. Optical recognition of characters in printed documents.
glossary.perizia_def: Technical document produced by a forensic examiner, containing the analysis and conclusions for a case.
glossary.embedding_def: Numerical vector representation of text, used for semantic search in the knowledge base.

requirements.title: Requirements & Configuration
requirements.ollama_desc: GraphoLab requires Ollama running locally. Download it from ollama.com and start it before using the app.
requirements.hw_ram: RAM: minimum 8 GB (16 GB or more recommended for larger models)
requirements.hw_disk: Disk space: 5–15 GB for Ollama models
requirements.openai_desc: You can configure an OpenAI API key in Settings to use GPT models as an alternative to local models. The key is encrypted and never transmitted in plain text.

privacy.title: Privacy & Data
privacy.local_desc: All uploaded documents are processed entirely on the local server. No file or data is sent to third-party services.
privacy.openai_desc: If you configure an OpenAI API key, requests to GPT models are sent to OpenAI servers. Ensure you comply with OpenAI's usage policies for forensic data.
privacy.storage_desc: Documents are stored locally (MinIO/filesystem). API keys are encrypted in the database using symmetric encryption.
privacy.audit_desc: All operations are recorded in the audit log, accessible to administrators. This helps ensure traceability of forensic analyses.
